projectName: Al Ghurair Customer Engagement & Marketing Platform
description:
A comprehensive Salesforce-based customer engagement and marketing automation platform for Al Ghurair Group's four business units (Al Ghurair Centre, Property Management, Glitch, Flayva). The solution integrates Service Cloud for omnichannel contact center operations with Marketing Cloud for multi-channel campaign management, unified by a Customer Data Platform (CDP) to create a single customer view across all touchpoints. The platform will support 8 agents with integrations to Genesys CTI, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox, and multiple peripheral systems.

Cost Breakdown (23 line items):
  Blueprint / Discovery Workshops & Requirements Analysis: 216h / 27.0 MD — Multi-business unit discovery sessions covering Service Cloud requirements, Marketing Cloud needs, CDP strategy, contact center operations, campaign management, and integration requirements across 4 business units. Up to 12 workshop sessions with key stakeholders.
  Blueprint / Solution Architecture & Design: 248.4h / 31.1 MD — Technical architecture for Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, CDP integration layer, multi-org strategy, data synchronization framework, security model across 4 business units, and API integration architecture for 6+ external systems (Genesys, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox, WiFi, Footfall).
  Blueprint / Data Model & CDP Strategy Design: 237.6h / 29.7 MD — Unified customer data model design, CDP data schema, customer matching and deduplication rules, data governance framework, consent management model, unified profile design across 4 business units, and data migration strategy from existing sources.
  Blueprint / Integration Specifications: 248.4h / 31.1 MD — Detailed integration specifications for Genesys CTI, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox loyalty system, mall WiFi solution, footfall tracking solution. API contracts, data mapping, error handling, sync frequency documentation, and authentication mechanisms.
  Blueprint / Project Planning & Documentation: 144h / 18.0 MD — Detailed project plan with phase breakdowns, resource allocation across 6 phases, sprint planning for agile delivery, risk register, RAID log, detailed user stories with acceptance criteria, epics breakdown, governance model documentation, and stakeholder communication plan.
  Implementation / Service Cloud Foundation Setup: 205.2h / 25.6 MD — Core Salesforce org provisioning, role hierarchy for 4 business units, profile and permission set configuration (up to 12 profiles, 20 permission sets), custom object model setup for multi-BU requirements, security model, business hours (4 schedules), holidays, and basic automation.
  Implementation / Account & Contact Management: 151.2h / 18.9 MD — Multi-BU account and contact configuration, custom fields (up to 80 fields), record types for different business units (up to 4), page layouts, relationship model between customers and BUs, VIP flagging, consolidated timeline view, and 360° customer profile.
  Implementation / Case Management & Omnichannel Console: 291.6h / 36.5 MD — Case object configuration with record types for each BU, complex case routing logic, queue configuration per department, email-to-case setup (up to 4 mailboxes), web-to-case forms, Service Console customization with 360° view, omnichannel setup for voice/email/WhatsApp/chat, agent workload management, and SLA tracking.
  Implementation / Knowledge Base & Self-Service: 97.2h / 12.2 MD — Knowledge base setup with category structure for each BU, article templates (up to 15), FAQ content migration (up to 100 articles provided by client), agent scripts, quick text templates (up to 25), search optimization, and knowledge article workflow.
  Implementation / Marketing Cloud Setup & Configuration: 334.8h / 41.9 MD — Marketing Cloud provisioning, business unit setup for 4 entities, data extensions design, contact model configuration, email studio setup with templates (up to 25), mobile studio configuration for SMS and WhatsApp (promotional), journey builder foundation, sender profiles, and authentication.
  Implementation / Customer Data Platform (CDP) Implementation: 432h / 54.0 MD — CDP core setup and configuration, unified customer profile design across 4 BUs, data source connections (15,000 contacts), identity resolution rules, customer matching and deduplication, data model implementation, consent management, real-time profile updates from all touchpoints, and data governance.
  Implementation / Genesys CTI Integration: 259.2h / 32.4 MD — Genesys Cloud/Finesse integration with Salesforce Service Cloud using pre-built connector (Bucher+Suter, 2Ring, or NovelVox), screen pop configuration, click-to-dial, automatic call logging, call recording integration, presence synchronization, agent state management, and supervisor dashboard integration. Connector licenses not included.
  Implementation / Peripheral Systems Integration: 302.4h / 37.8 MD — Integration with Semnox loyalty/gaming system, mall WiFi solution for customer tracking, footfall tracking solution for visitor analytics. Real-time data sync, customer activity tracking, unified profile enrichment, and transactional data flow. REST API integrations with error handling and retry logic.
  Implementation / Qualtrics & Sprinklr Integration: 205.2h / 25.6 MD — Qualtrics integration for customer feedback surveys with automatic case creation from negative sentiment, NPS tracking, survey distribution via email/SMS. Sprinklr integration for social media engagement tracking (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok), case creation from social channels, unified customer communication history, and sentiment analysis.
  Implementation / Bot & Agentforce Automation: 313.2h / 39.1 MD — Einstein Bot configuration for FAQ handling (100 articles), automated responses for common queries across 4 BUs, chatbot training with knowledge base integration, intelligent handoff to live agents, Agentforce setup for AI-driven case routing, automated customer query classification, and agent productivity tools.
  Implementation / Campaign Automation & Journey Builder: 280.8h / 35.1 MD — Marketing Cloud Journey Builder configuration for automated campaigns across email, SMS, WhatsApp. Multi-channel journey design (16 email campaigns, 16 SMS campaigns, 36 WhatsApp campaigns annually), personalization setup using CDP data, AI-driven send time optimization (Einstein), A/B testing configuration, campaign performance tracking, and engagement scoring.
  Implementation / Advanced Segmentation & Audience Builder: 259.2h / 32.4 MD — Customer segmentation engine configuration in CDP, audience builder setup for dynamic segments, segment creation based on behavior/demographics/transactions (15,000 contacts, 10% YoY growth), cross-business unit segmentation, predictive audiences using AI (Einstein), segment activation across channels, and lookalike audience modeling.
  Implementation / Analytics & Reporting Framework: 216h / 27.0 MD — Service Cloud dashboards (agent performance, case metrics, SLA tracking, channel distribution, 8 agent KPIs), Marketing Cloud analytics (campaign ROI, engagement metrics, channel performance for email/SMS/WhatsApp), CDP analytics (customer lifetime value, segmentation insights, cross-BU behavior), cross-platform reporting with up to 25 custom reports and 8 executive dashboards for management.
  UAT & Go-Live / User Acceptance Testing: 331.2h / 41.4 MD — Comprehensive UAT planning and execution, test scenario creation covering all 4 business units and use cases, UAT environment setup, test data preparation (15,000 contacts), defect tracking and resolution in Jira, regression testing post-fixes, performance testing, and user signoff documentation from each BU.
  UAT & Go-Live / Training & Enablement: 104h / 13.0 MD — Training material development (presentations, user guides, video tutorials), agent training (8 agents, 2 team leaders) for Service Cloud and omnichannel operations, admin training (1 admin, 1 platform owner) for system configuration, marketing user training for Marketing Cloud and Journey Builder, train-the-trainer sessions. Total 8 training sessions (2 sessions per group).
  UAT & Go-Live / Documentation: 110.4h / 13.8 MD — Comprehensive system documentation including technical architecture diagrams, configuration guides for Service Cloud and Marketing Cloud, integration documentation for all 6 systems, user manuals for agents and admins, SOPs for each business unit, troubleshooting guides, video tutorials (10-15 videos), and knowledge base article creation.
  UAT & Go-Live / Production Deployment: 183.6h / 22.9 MD — Production environment configuration and validation, data migration execution (15,000 contacts with deduplication), integration cutover for all 6 systems, production validation testing, go-live checklist execution, rollback planning and procedures, production monitoring setup, and performance validation.
  UAT & Go-Live / Hypercare Support: 183.6h / 22.9 MD — Post-go-live support for 3 weeks including L1/L2 issue resolution, production monitoring, user support for 8 agents and 2 team leaders, minor configuration adjustments, knowledge base updates based on live usage, performance tuning, and handover documentation to BAU support team.

Assumptions: All required Salesforce licenses (Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, CDP/Data Cloud, Digital Engagement for WhatsApp) will be procured by client before implementation phase starts; Third-party system licenses (Genesys, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox) are already in place with active subscriptions and accessible APIs; APIs for all 6 external systems are documented and available with dedicated test environments for integration development and testing; Client will provide dedicated business SMEs from each of the 4 business units (Mall, Property Management, Glitch, Flayva) for workshops, requirements validation, and UAT; Maximum 15,000 customer contacts to be migrated initially with 10% year-over-year growth. Data migration is minimal or out of scope for MVP; Knowledge base content (up to 100 articles) will be provided by client in ready-to-publish format for migration into Salesforce Knowledge; Contact center will have 8 agents, 2 team leaders, 1 admin, and 1 platform owner from IT. No additional headcount changes during implementation; All project work will be conducted remotely in English language. No on-site presence required in Dubai/UAE; Up to 25 email templates, 25 quick text templates, 20 permission sets, 12 profiles, and 4 business hour schedules are within scope; Campaign volumes as specified: 16 email campaigns, 16 SMS campaigns, 36 WhatsApp campaigns annually with 240K email sends, 240K SMS sends, 540K WhatsApp sends; Marketing Cloud edition (Corporate or Pro) supports the estimated send volumes without overage charges; Standard business hours configuration with UAE public holidays. Up to 4 different business hour schedules for different teams or BUs; Data migration from existing systems is considered out of scope or minimal for MVP launch. Historical data migration can be addressed post-launch if needed; UAT environment (sandbox) and production environment will be provisioned by client with appropriate storage and API limits; Major Salesforce platform upgrades (seasonal releases) during implementation period will not require significant rework or timeline extension; Genesys technical resources will be available for joint integration workshops, testing, and support during CTI integration phase; Pre-built CTI connector from Bucher+Suter, 2Ring, or NovelVox will be procured separately by client. Connector licensing costs are not included in this estimate; META Business Account for WhatsApp is already established or will be set up by client during Blueprint phase with phone number verification completed; Client has necessary approvals and compliance clearances for promotional marketing via email, SMS, and WhatsApp in UAE market; Project start date is flexible and can be scheduled with 2-3 weeks notice after contract signing and license procurement

projectName: Al Ghurair Customer Engagement & Marketing Platform
description:
A comprehensive Salesforce-based customer engagement and marketing automation platform for Al Ghurair Group's four business units (Al Ghurair Centre, Property Management, Glitch, Flayva). The solution integrates Service Cloud for omnichannel contact center operations with Marketing Cloud for multi-channel campaign management, unified by a Customer Data Platform (CDP) to create a single customer view across all touchpoints. The platform will support 8 agents with integrations to Genesys CTI, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox, and multiple peripheral systems.

Cost Breakdown (23 line items):
  Blueprint / Discovery Workshops & Requirements Analysis: 216h / 27.0 MD — Multi-business unit discovery sessions covering Service Cloud requirements, Marketing Cloud needs, CDP strategy, contact center operations, campaign management, and integration requirements across 4 business units. Up to 12 workshop sessions with key stakeholders.
  Blueprint / Solution Architecture & Design: 248.4h / 31.1 MD — Technical architecture for Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, CDP integration layer, multi-org strategy, data synchronization framework, security model across 4 business units, and API integration architecture for 6+ external systems (Genesys, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox, WiFi, Footfall).
  Blueprint / Data Model & CDP Strategy Design: 237.6h / 29.7 MD — Unified customer data model design, CDP data schema, customer matching and deduplication rules, data governance framework, consent management model, unified profile design across 4 business units, and data migration strategy from existing sources.
  Blueprint / Integration Specifications: 248.4h / 31.1 MD — Detailed integration specifications for Genesys CTI, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox loyalty system, mall WiFi solution, footfall tracking solution. API contracts, data mapping, error handling, sync frequency documentation, and authentication mechanisms.
  Blueprint / Project Planning & Documentation: 144h / 18.0 MD — Detailed project plan with phase breakdowns, resource allocation across 6 phases, sprint planning for agile delivery, risk register, RAID log, detailed user stories with acceptance criteria, epics breakdown, governance model documentation, and stakeholder communication plan.
  Implementation / Service Cloud Foundation Setup: 205.2h / 25.6 MD — Core Salesforce org provisioning, role hierarchy for 4 business units, profile and permission set configuration (up to 12 profiles, 20 permission sets), custom object model setup for multi-BU requirements, security model, business hours (4 schedules), holidays, and basic automation.
  Implementation / Account & Contact Management: 151.2h / 18.9 MD — Multi-BU account and contact configuration, custom fields (up to 80 fields), record types for different business units (up to 4), page layouts, relationship model between customers and BUs, VIP flagging, consolidated timeline view, and 360° customer profile.
  Implementation / Case Management & Omnichannel Console: 291.6h / 36.5 MD — Case object configuration with record types for each BU, complex case routing logic, queue configuration per department, email-to-case setup (up to 4 mailboxes), web-to-case forms, Service Console customization with 360° view, omnichannel setup for voice/email/WhatsApp/chat, agent workload management, and SLA tracking.
  Implementation / Knowledge Base & Self-Service: 97.2h / 12.2 MD — Knowledge base setup with category structure for each BU, article templates (up to 15), FAQ content migration (up to 100 articles provided by client), agent scripts, quick text templates (up to 25), search optimization, and knowledge article workflow.
  Implementation / Marketing Cloud Setup & Configuration: 334.8h / 41.9 MD — Marketing Cloud provisioning, business unit setup for 4 entities, data extensions design, contact model configuration, email studio setup with templates (up to 25), mobile studio configuration for SMS and WhatsApp (promotional), journey builder foundation, sender profiles, and authentication.
  Implementation / Customer Data Platform (CDP) Implementation: 432h / 54.0 MD — CDP core setup and configuration, unified customer profile design across 4 BUs, data source connections (15,000 contacts), identity resolution rules, customer matching and deduplication, data model implementation, consent management, real-time profile updates from all touchpoints, and data governance.
  Implementation / Genesys CTI Integration: 259.2h / 32.4 MD — Genesys Cloud/Finesse integration with Salesforce Service Cloud using pre-built connector (Bucher+Suter, 2Ring, or NovelVox), screen pop configuration, click-to-dial, automatic call logging, call recording integration, presence synchronization, agent state management, and supervisor dashboard integration. Connector licenses not included.
  Implementation / Peripheral Systems Integration: 302.4h / 37.8 MD — Integration with Semnox loyalty/gaming system, mall WiFi solution for customer tracking, footfall tracking solution for visitor analytics. Real-time data sync, customer activity tracking, unified profile enrichment, and transactional data flow. REST API integrations with error handling and retry logic.
  Implementation / Qualtrics & Sprinklr Integration: 205.2h / 25.6 MD — Qualtrics integration for customer feedback surveys with automatic case creation from negative sentiment, NPS tracking, survey distribution via email/SMS. Sprinklr integration for social media engagement tracking (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok), case creation from social channels, unified customer communication history, and sentiment analysis.
  Implementation / Bot & Agentforce Automation: 313.2h / 39.1 MD — Einstein Bot configuration for FAQ handling (100 articles), automated responses for common queries across 4 BUs, chatbot training with knowledge base integration, intelligent handoff to live agents, Agentforce setup for AI-driven case routing, automated customer query classification, and agent productivity tools.
  Implementation / Campaign Automation & Journey Builder: 280.8h / 35.1 MD — Marketing Cloud Journey Builder configuration for automated campaigns across email, SMS, WhatsApp. Multi-channel journey design (16 email campaigns, 16 SMS campaigns, 36 WhatsApp campaigns annually), personalization setup using CDP data, AI-driven send time optimization (Einstein), A/B testing configuration, campaign performance tracking, and engagement scoring.
  Implementation / Advanced Segmentation & Audience Builder: 259.2h / 32.4 MD — Customer segmentation engine configuration in CDP, audience builder setup for dynamic segments, segment creation based on behavior/demographics/transactions (15,000 contacts, 10% YoY growth), cross-business unit segmentation, predictive audiences using AI (Einstein), segment activation across channels, and lookalike audience modeling.
  Implementation / Analytics & Reporting Framework: 216h / 27.0 MD — Service Cloud dashboards (agent performance, case metrics, SLA tracking, channel distribution, 8 agent KPIs), Marketing Cloud analytics (campaign ROI, engagement metrics, channel performance for email/SMS/WhatsApp), CDP analytics (customer lifetime value, segmentation insights, cross-BU behavior), cross-platform reporting with up to 25 custom reports and 8 executive dashboards for management.
  UAT & Go-Live / User Acceptance Testing: 331.2h / 41.4 MD — Comprehensive UAT planning and execution, test scenario creation covering all 4 business units and use cases, UAT environment setup, test data preparation (15,000 contacts), defect tracking and resolution in Jira, regression testing post-fixes, performance testing, and user signoff documentation from each BU.
  UAT & Go-Live / Training & Enablement: 104h / 13.0 MD — Training material development (presentations, user guides, video tutorials), agent training (8 agents, 2 team leaders) for Service Cloud and omnichannel operations, admin training (1 admin, 1 platform owner) for system configuration, marketing user training for Marketing Cloud and Journey Builder, train-the-trainer sessions. Total 8 training sessions (2 sessions per group).
  UAT & Go-Live / Documentation: 110.4h / 13.8 MD — Comprehensive system documentation including technical architecture diagrams, configuration guides for Service Cloud and Marketing Cloud, integration documentation for all 6 systems, user manuals for agents and admins, SOPs for each business unit, troubleshooting guides, video tutorials (10-15 videos), and knowledge base article creation.
  UAT & Go-Live / Production Deployment: 183.6h / 22.9 MD — Production environment configuration and validation, data migration execution (15,000 contacts with deduplication), integration cutover for all 6 systems, production validation testing, go-live checklist execution, rollback planning and procedures, production monitoring setup, and performance validation.
  UAT & Go-Live / Hypercare Support: 183.6h / 22.9 MD — Post-go-live support for 3 weeks including L1/L2 issue resolution, production monitoring, user support for 8 agents and 2 team leaders, minor configuration adjustments, knowledge base updates based on live usage, performance tuning, and handover documentation to BAU support team.

Assumptions: All required Salesforce licenses (Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, CDP/Data Cloud, Digital Engagement for WhatsApp) will be procured by client before implementation phase starts; Third-party system licenses (Genesys, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox) are already in place with active subscriptions and accessible APIs; APIs for all 6 external systems are documented and available with dedicated test environments for integration development and testing; Client will provide dedicated business SMEs from each of the 4 business units (Mall, Property Management, Glitch, Flayva) for workshops, requirements validation, and UAT; Maximum 15,000 customer contacts to be migrated initially with 10% year-over-year growth. Data migration is minimal or out of scope for MVP; Knowledge base content (up to 100 articles) will be provided by client in ready-to-publish format for migration into Salesforce Knowledge; Contact center will have 8 agents, 2 team leaders, 1 admin, and 1 platform owner from IT. No additional headcount changes during implementation; All project work will be conducted remotely in English language. No on-site presence required in Dubai/UAE; Up to 25 email templates, 25 quick text templates, 20 permission sets, 12 profiles, and 4 business hour schedules are within scope; Campaign volumes as specified: 16 email campaigns, 16 SMS campaigns, 36 WhatsApp campaigns annually with 240K email sends, 240K SMS sends, 540K WhatsApp sends; Marketing Cloud edition (Corporate or Pro) supports the estimated send volumes without overage charges; Standard business hours configuration with UAE public holidays. Up to 4 different business hour schedules for different teams or BUs; Data migration from existing systems is considered out of scope or minimal for MVP launch. Historical data migration can be addressed post-launch if needed; UAT environment (sandbox) and production environment will be provisioned by client with appropriate storage and API limits; Major Salesforce platform upgrades (seasonal releases) during implementation period will not require significant rework or timeline extension; Genesys technical resources will be available for joint integration workshops, testing, and support during CTI integration phase; Pre-built CTI connector from Bucher+Suter, 2Ring, or NovelVox will be procured separately by client. Connector licensing costs are not included in this estimate; META Business Account for WhatsApp is already established or will be set up by client during Blueprint phase with phone number verification completed; Client has necessary approvals and compliance clearances for promotional marketing via email, SMS, and WhatsApp in UAE market; Project start date is flexible and can be scheduled with 2-3 weeks notice after contract signing and license procurement

description:
A comprehensive e-commerce web application built with React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL, featuring product catalog management, shopping cart, Stripe payment integration, order tracking, and admin dashboard. The platform targets small to medium businesses selling physical products online with a clean, responsive interface. Estimated delivery in 22 weeks with a small, experienced team.

Cost Breakdown (18 line items):
  Blueprint / Requirements Analysis & Discovery: 92h / 11.5 MD — Stakeholder interviews, business requirements gathering, competitive analysis, feature prioritization, and functional specification documentation
  Blueprint / Technical Architecture & Database Design: 101h / 12.6 MD — System architecture design, technology stack validation, database schema design, API architecture, scalability planning, and security framework definition
  Blueprint / UX/UI Design: 129h / 16.1 MD — User research, wireframing, high-fidelity mockups, design system creation, responsive design layouts, brand integration, and prototype development
  Blueprint / Project Planning & Setup: 72h / 9.0 MD — Sprint planning, development environment setup, CI/CD pipeline configuration, project management tools setup, and team onboarding
  Implementation / User Authentication & Account Management: 151h / 18.9 MD — User registration, login/logout, password reset, email verification, profile management, session management, and JWT implementation
  Implementation / Product Catalog & Search System: 205h / 25.6 MD — Product listing pages, product detail pages, category hierarchy, advanced search with filters, pagination, product image management, and SEO optimization
  Implementation / Shopping Cart & Checkout: 162h / 20.3 MD — Cart management (add/remove/update), cart persistence, checkout flow, guest checkout option, shipping address forms, order summary, and cart abandonment handling
  Implementation / Payment Integration & Order Processing: 151h / 18.9 MD — Stripe payment gateway integration, payment form, payment processing, order creation, payment confirmation, refund handling, and PCI compliance measures
  Implementation / Order Management & Tracking: 129h / 16.1 MD — Order history, order details view, order status tracking, order confirmation emails, shipping notifications, and customer order dashboard
  Implementation / Admin Dashboard & Inventory Management: 227h / 28.4 MD — Admin authentication, product CRUD operations, inventory tracking, bulk product upload, category management, order management interface, sales analytics, and user management
  Implementation / Email Notifications & SendGrid Integration: 87h / 10.9 MD — SendGrid integration, email templates, order confirmation emails, shipping notifications, password reset emails, welcome emails, and email logging
  Implementation / Responsive Design & Mobile Optimization: 151h / 18.9 MD — Mobile-first responsive layouts, cross-browser testing, touch interaction optimization, performance optimization for mobile, and progressive web app features
  Implementation / Security Implementation & Data Protection: 141h / 17.6 MD — Input validation, SQL injection prevention, XSS protection, CSRF tokens, secure headers, data encryption, GDPR compliance measures, and security audit
  UAT & Go-Live / User Acceptance Testing: 152h / 19.0 MD — Test case creation, UAT execution with stakeholders, functionality verification, usability testing, cross-browser testing, and mobile device testing
  UAT & Go-Live / Bug Fixes & Refinements: 138h / 17.3 MD — Critical and high-priority bug resolution, performance optimization, UI/UX refinements based on feedback, and final code review
  UAT & Go-Live / Production Deployment & Infrastructure: 108h / 13.5 MD — Production server setup, database migration, SSL certificate configuration, CDN setup, monitoring tools configuration, backup procedures, and go-live execution
  UAT & Go-Live / Documentation & Training: 96h / 12.0 MD — Technical documentation, API documentation, admin user guide, end-user help documentation, video tutorials, and admin training sessions
  UAT & Go-Live / Post-Launch Support: 80h / 10.0 MD — 30-day post-launch monitoring, immediate bug fixes, performance monitoring, user support, and knowledge transfer to client team

Assumptions: Client will provide all product content (descriptions, images, pricing) in a structured format ready for import; A single default currency (USD) and language (English) will be supported initially; multi-currency/language is out of scope; Stripe will be used in standard mode; custom payment flows or advanced fraud detection features are not included; Product catalog will contain fewer than 10,000 SKUs at launch; larger catalogs may require additional optimization; Client has existing domain name and will handle DNS configuration with provided guidance; Shipping calculation will be basic (flat rate, free shipping thresholds); complex carrier integrations are out of scope; Tax calculation will be simple percentage-based; integration with tax services like Avalara is not included; Client will provide brand assets (logo, colors, fonts) and any existing brand guidelines; Standard web hosting environment will be sufficient; specialized compliance requirements (e.g., HIPAA) are not needed; Post-launch support covers bug fixes and minor adjustments, not new feature development

description:
1 Discovery and solution design stage (23 MD)
  1.1. Process design workshops (23 MD)
    Deliverables:
- Service Cloud | up to 5 Sessions
- Integrations: TPA system, CTI,  Email | up to 5 Sessions

- High Level Architecture
- Epics + User Stories + Acceptance Criteria
- Project Plan
    Assumption: Workshop preparation and workshops with key business users on the following topics:
- Customer Care & Case Management
- Integration and Data Migration
2 Quick Phase 1 (68.2 MD)
  2.1 Basic Org Setup (7 MD)
    - Configuration of Role Hierarchy, Profiles
- Configure main App for Service team 
- Configure standard and custom objects with fields, record types and page layouts for the main entities
- Users creation
- Email/ event  integration (Exchange/Outlook)
- Audit Trails
    Assumption: - Up to 5 Profiles
- Up to 5 permission sets
- The system will be configured in English language.
- Einstein Activity Capture for email tracking Configuration (Microsoft Exchange must meet the requirements https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.aac_system_requirements.htm&type=5)
- Control access to ticket creation, editing, and viewing by user role.
- Restrict access to certain ticket types or caller types (e.g., call center cannot view client relation tickets).
- Limit complaint handling to specific profiles with elevated privileges.
- Ensure users can only view activities for caller types they are authorized for
- Security by provider, payer and/or broker. Users are allowed to view all tickets and contacts related to specific payers or providers or brokers.
- Log all changes to tickets, comments, and assignments for compliance purposes
- Log all accesses on tickets, searches for contacts for compliance purposes
  2.2 Account Managament (3.8 MD)
    - Display member profile with name, policy, insurer, group, and insurance
product. This information can be retrieved from core TPA system in real time
through API calls.
- Show VIP flag prominently if member is marked VIP
- Display most recent approvals and their statuses with ability to view
comments/reasons for rejection
- Show as related provider profile including code and organization details.
-Display as related to customer insurer/broker account and department
- Display most recent tickets and escalations
    Assumption: - Specific Queue per Department (clear number of departments will be clarified during the Discovery sessions) 
- Each department and entity will have customized case types and categories and specific rules for assignment
- Up to 3 Record types for Accounts
- Consolidated timeline of all interactions (calls, emails, complaints, chat)
- Up to 50 custom fields
  2.3 Case Management (26.4 MD)
    - Each department and entity must have a dedicated mailbox for case management
- Each specific Case Type could have a specific record type with a specific layout 
- Web to Case 
- Survey
- Complex routing logic for incoming tickets/ cases and calls
- Case statuses: Open, Pending, Resolved, Closed
- Complaint Management
- Notifications & Communication to customers from Case via chats
    Assumption: - Specific Queue per Department (clear number of departments will be clarified during the Discovery sessions) 
- Each department and entity will have customized case types and categories and specific rules for assignment
- Case visibility setup (role hierarchy)  
- Up to 3 Record types for Case
- Up to 3 different emails for Email to Case 
- Up to 25 Email Templates for agent answers
- Up to 10 Quick Text templates
- Up to 10 flow automation for routing logic
- Multiple case sources: telephony, email, manual entry, webform, chat
- Capture detailed complaint attributes to meet regulatory requirements:
a) Resolution Types: Fully upheld, Partially upheld, Denied.
b) Complainant Satisfaction Outcome: 1 = fully satisfied, 2 = largely
satisfied, 3 = largely unsatisfied, 4 = completely dissatisfied
- Allow agents to send email/SMS/Chat updates from within CRM;
automatically log them as activities.
- Assignment : support manual assignment automated assignment (using round-robin, loadbased, or skill-based routing, type of ticket, payer, provider)
-  Ability to configure assignment models per group/team
  2.4 Entitelment Process Setup (16.3 MD)
    - Service Agreements Tracking
- SLA process setup 
- Eligibility Verification
- Case Automation
- Milestone Management 
- Business Hours & Holidays
    Assumption: - Setup up tp 2 Entitlement Processes
- Setup specific First Response Time, the time by which the first response to a customer inquiry must occur
- Setup specific Resolution Time, the time by which the case must be resolved.
- Setup escalation notification(s) & logic based on SLA breach ( escalation to supervisor or Manager) 
- SLA Reset (SLA reset status when a team member takes ownership of the ticket by changing the status from New to In progress Status)
- Parent tickets cannot be closed until the child tickets are closed
- Define multiple business hours schedules (per team or per line of business)
- Configure holidays and non-working days per team; SLAs should respect these calendars
  2.5 Knowledge Base (3.5 MD)
    - Each department and entity will have access to FAQs, a knowledge base, and content tailored to their operations
    Assumption: - T1A team will upload and publish all articales whoch will be provided by customer 
- Topics management
- Medium level complexity
- Up to 50 articles
- Agent scripts and pre-approved quick replies (e.g., standard wording for policy
exclusions or network downtimes)
  2.6 Omni Channel Console Setup (4.2 MD)
    - Setup a Service Console specifically dedicated to the Service team.
    Assumption: - The flag and whisper message
- Omni Supervisor view 
- CTI application setup
- Recording option 
- Visibility into current workload, assigned work versus unassigned work and
workload
  2.7 Digital Engagement (2.8 MD)
    - Whatsapp channel sync with META Business Account
    Assumption: -  It is supposed to setup Digital Engagement for interactions through WhatsApp
- Additional cost of Digital Engagement licenses is not included into current estimation
  2.8 Reports and Dashboards incl Revenue Cloud reports (4.2 MD)
    - Report and dashboard will be customizable to reflect the performance metrics and operational insights of each department and entity.
    Assumption: - Up to 10 reports and 15 dashboards based on those reports
4 Integrations (31.2 MD)
  Assumption: Data Migration is not in the project scope
  4.1 TPA System (24.5 MD)
    Synchronization of
- Accounts & Contacts
- Approvals 
- Claims
    Assumption: - Binary, real-time API integrations with core TPA system 
- Up to 5 flows 
- REST API 
- Salesforce will initiate API call based on changes on its side and sending the updates to TPA system
- TPA validates, saves, and returns result 
- details of integrations flows and data model will be clarified during discovery workshops to ensure there is a clear flow between two systems
  4.2 Integrate with CISCO Finess (6.8 MD)
    - CTI & Salesforce connection
    Assumption: - There is no native, out-of-the-box connector provided by Salesforce or Cisco specifically for Cisco Finesse
- Use pre-built, AppExchange-certified connectors from companies like Bucher + Suter, 2Ring, or NovelVox. Licneses not icnluded in price 
- Using more advanced Service Cloud Voice. Licneses not icnluded in price
6 Documentation (2.2 MD)
  Video guides in English. User guide contains information about how to utilize the system for a daily job
7 Training (5.5 MD)
  4 training sessions for admin and agent training to enable internal teams to manage users
  Assumption: Training preparation and 4 remote training session in English for 2 group of key users, and admin. Max 12 people in the group
8 User Acceptance Testing (11.6 MD)
  UAT preparation and UAT support
  Assumption: - Overall 1 full UAT process for each Go Live Release
9 Release from Sandbox to Production org (6.6 MD)
  Transfer functionality from Sandbox to Production environment and activation on Production
  Assumption: Settings migration between 2 environments - Sandbox and Production
10 Support after Go-Live (11 MD)
  Support during 2 weeks after Go-Live

projectName: Al Ghurair Customer Engagement & Marketing Platform
description:
A comprehensive Salesforce-based customer engagement and marketing automation platform for Al Ghurair Group's four business units (Al Ghurair Centre, Property Management, Glitch, Flayva). The solution integrates Service Cloud for omnichannel contact center operations with Marketing Cloud for multi-channel campaign management, unified by a Customer Data Platform (CDP) to create a single customer view across all touchpoints. The platform will support 8 agents with integrations to Genesys CTI, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox, and multiple peripheral systems.

Cost Breakdown (23 line items):
  Blueprint / Discovery Workshops & Requirements Analysis: 204h / 25.5 MD — Multi-business unit discovery sessions covering Service Cloud requirements, Marketing Cloud needs, CDP strategy, contact center operations, campaign management, and integration requirements across 4 business units. Up to 12 workshop sessions with key stakeholders.
  Blueprint / Solution Architecture & Design: 204.39999999999998h / 25.5 MD — Technical architecture for Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, CDP integration layer, multi-org strategy, data synchronization framework, security model across 4 business units, and API integration architecture for 6+ external systems (Genesys, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox, WiFi, Footfall).
  Blueprint / Data Model & CDP Strategy Design: 237.6h / 29.7 MD — Unified customer data model design, CDP data schema, customer matching and deduplication rules, data governance framework, consent management model, unified profile design across 4 business units, and data migration strategy from existing sources.
  Blueprint / Integration Specifications: 190.39999999999998h / 23.8 MD — Detailed integration specifications for Genesys CTI, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox loyalty system, mall WiFi solution, footfall tracking solution. API contracts, data mapping, error handling, sync frequency documentation, and authentication mechanisms.
  Blueprint / Project Planning & Documentation: 144h / 18.0 MD — Detailed project plan with phase breakdowns, resource allocation across 6 phases, sprint planning for agile delivery, risk register, RAID log, detailed user stories with acceptance criteria, epics breakdown, governance model documentation, and stakeholder communication plan.
  Implementation / Service Cloud Foundation Setup: 205.2h / 25.6 MD — Core Salesforce org provisioning, role hierarchy for 4 business units, profile and permission set configuration (up to 12 profiles, 20 permission sets), custom object model setup for multi-BU requirements, security model, business hours (4 schedules), holidays, and basic automation.
  Implementation / Account & Contact Management: 151.2h / 18.9 MD — Multi-BU account and contact configuration, custom fields (up to 80 fields), record types for different business units (up to 4), page layouts, relationship model between customers and BUs, VIP flagging, consolidated timeline view, and 360° customer profile.
  Implementation / Case Management & Omnichannel Console: 291.6h / 36.5 MD — Case object configuration with record types for each BU, complex case routing logic, queue configuration per department, email-to-case setup (up to 4 mailboxes), web-to-case forms, Service Console customization with 360° view, omnichannel setup for voice/email/WhatsApp/chat, agent workload management, and SLA tracking.
  Implementation / Knowledge Base & Self-Service: 97.2h / 12.2 MD — Knowledge base setup with category structure for each BU, article templates (up to 15), FAQ content migration (up to 100 articles provided by client), agent scripts, quick text templates (up to 25), search optimization, and knowledge article workflow.
  Implementation / Marketing Cloud Setup & Configuration: 334.8h / 41.9 MD — Marketing Cloud provisioning, business unit setup for 4 entities, data extensions design, contact model configuration, email studio setup with templates (up to 25), mobile studio configuration for SMS and WhatsApp (promotional), journey builder foundation, sender profiles, and authentication.
  Implementation / Customer Data Platform (CDP) Implementation: 432h / 54.0 MD — CDP core setup and configuration, unified customer profile design across 4 BUs, data source connections (15,000 contacts), identity resolution rules, customer matching and deduplication, data model implementation, consent management, real-time profile updates from all touchpoints, and data governance.
  Implementation / Genesys CTI Integration: 259.2h / 32.4 MD — Genesys Cloud/Finesse integration with Salesforce Service Cloud using pre-built connector (Bucher+Suter, 2Ring, or NovelVox), screen pop configuration, click-to-dial, automatic call logging, call recording integration, presence synchronization, agent state management, and supervisor dashboard integration. Connector licenses not included.
  Implementation / Peripheral Systems Integration: 302.4h / 37.8 MD — Integration with Semnox loyalty/gaming system, mall WiFi solution for customer tracking, footfall tracking solution for visitor analytics. Real-time data sync, customer activity tracking, unified profile enrichment, and transactional data flow. REST API integrations with error handling and retry logic.
  Implementation / Qualtrics & Sprinklr Integration: 205.2h / 25.6 MD — Qualtrics integration for customer feedback surveys with automatic case creation from negative sentiment, NPS tracking, survey distribution via email/SMS. Sprinklr integration for social media engagement tracking (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok), case creation from social channels, unified customer communication history, and sentiment analysis.
  Implementation / Bot & Agentforce Automation: 313.2h / 39.1 MD — Einstein Bot configuration for FAQ handling (100 articles), automated responses for common queries across 4 BUs, chatbot training with knowledge base integration, intelligent handoff to live agents, Agentforce setup for AI-driven case routing, automated customer query classification, and agent productivity tools.
  Implementation / Campaign Automation & Journey Builder: 280.8h / 35.1 MD — Marketing Cloud Journey Builder configuration for automated campaigns across email, SMS, WhatsApp. Multi-channel journey design (16 email campaigns, 16 SMS campaigns, 36 WhatsApp campaigns annually), personalization setup using CDP data, AI-driven send time optimization (Einstein), A/B testing configuration, campaign performance tracking, and engagement scoring.
  Implementation / Advanced Segmentation & Audience Builder: 259.2h / 32.4 MD — Customer segmentation engine configuration in CDP, audience builder setup for dynamic segments, segment creation based on behavior/demographics/transactions (15,000 contacts, 10% YoY growth), cross-business unit segmentation, predictive audiences using AI (Einstein), segment activation across channels, and lookalike audience modeling.
  Implementation / Analytics & Reporting Framework: 216h / 27.0 MD — Service Cloud dashboards (agent performance, case metrics, SLA tracking, channel distribution, 8 agent KPIs), Marketing Cloud analytics (campaign ROI, engagement metrics, channel performance for email/SMS/WhatsApp), CDP analytics (customer lifetime value, segmentation insights, cross-BU behavior), cross-platform reporting with up to 25 custom reports and 8 executive dashboards for management.
  UAT & Go-Live / User Acceptance Testing: 331.2h / 41.4 MD — Comprehensive UAT planning and execution, test scenario creation covering all 4 business units and use cases, UAT environment setup, test data preparation (15,000 contacts), defect tracking and resolution in Jira, regression testing post-fixes, performance testing, and user signoff documentation from each BU.
  UAT & Go-Live / Training & Enablement: 104h / 13.0 MD — Training material development (presentations, user guides, video tutorials), agent training (8 agents, 2 team leaders) for Service Cloud and omnichannel operations, admin training (1 admin, 1 platform owner) for system configuration, marketing user training for Marketing Cloud and Journey Builder, train-the-trainer sessions. Total 8 training sessions (2 sessions per group).
  UAT & Go-Live / Documentation: 110.4h / 13.8 MD — Comprehensive system documentation including technical architecture diagrams, configuration guides for Service Cloud and Marketing Cloud, integration documentation for all 6 systems, user manuals for agents and admins, SOPs for each business unit, troubleshooting guides, video tutorials (10-15 videos), and knowledge base article creation.
  UAT & Go-Live / Production Deployment: 183.6h / 22.9 MD — Production environment configuration and validation, data migration execution (15,000 contacts with deduplication), integration cutover for all 6 systems, production validation testing, go-live checklist execution, rollback planning and procedures, production monitoring setup, and performance validation.
  UAT & Go-Live / Hypercare Support: 183.6h / 22.9 MD — Post-go-live support for 3 weeks including L1/L2 issue resolution, production monitoring, user support for 8 agents and 2 team leaders, minor configuration adjustments, knowledge base updates based on live usage, performance tuning, and handover documentation to BAU support team.

Assumptions: All required Salesforce licenses (Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, CDP/Data Cloud, Digital Engagement for WhatsApp) will be procured by client before implementation phase starts; Third-party system licenses (Genesys, Qualtrics, Sprinklr, Semnox) are already in place with active subscriptions and accessible APIs; APIs for all 6 external systems are documented and available with dedicated test environments for integration development and testing; Client will provide dedicated business SMEs from each of the 4 business units (Mall, Property Management, Glitch, Flayva) for workshops, requirements validation, and UAT; Maximum 15,000 customer contacts to be migrated initially with 10% year-over-year growth. Data migration is minimal or out of scope for MVP; Knowledge base content (up to 100 articles) will be provided by client in ready-to-publish format for migration into Salesforce Knowledge; Contact center will have 8 agents, 2 team leaders, 1 admin, and 1 platform owner from IT. No additional headcount changes during implementation; All project work will be conducted remotely in English language. No on-site presence required in Dubai/UAE; Up to 25 email templates, 25 quick text templates, 20 permission sets, 12 profiles, and 4 business hour schedules are within scope; Campaign volumes as specified: 16 email campaigns, 16 SMS campaigns, 36 WhatsApp campaigns annually with 240K email sends, 240K SMS sends, 540K WhatsApp sends; Marketing Cloud edition (Corporate or Pro) supports the estimated send volumes without overage charges; Standard business hours configuration with UAE public holidays. Up to 4 different business hour schedules for different teams or BUs; Data migration from existing systems is considered out of scope or minimal for MVP launch. Historical data migration can be addressed post-launch if needed; UAT environment (sandbox) and production environment will be provisioned by client with appropriate storage and API limits; Major Salesforce platform upgrades (seasonal releases) during implementation period will not require significant rework or timeline extension; Genesys technical resources will be available for joint integration workshops, testing, and support during CTI integration phase; Pre-built CTI connector from Bucher+Suter, 2Ring, or NovelVox will be procured separately by client. Connector licensing costs are not included in this estimate; META Business Account for WhatsApp is already established or will be set up by client during Blueprint phase with phone number verification completed; Client has necessary approvals and compliance clearances for promotional marketing via email, SMS, and WhatsApp in UAE market; Project start date is flexible and can be scheduled with 2-3 weeks notice after contract signing and license procurement
